title: fix(gen-test): raise timeout for Bedrock-signing safety tests
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Problem
5 tests in `gen.pollinations.ai/test/safety.test.ts` flakily time out under the full-suite parallel run (`Test timed out in 5000ms`). The failing set is **non-deterministic** — across isolated re-runs of identical code on the same commit, 1–5 different tests fail, and one test passed at 5108ms in one run and timed out in another.

## Root cause
The `applySafety` / `applySafetyToChatRequest` tests sign requests with AWS SigV4 (WebCrypto HMAC-SHA256) inside the workerd runtime before reaching the (fully mocked, instant) `fetch`. That crypto path runs ~90ms warm but **6–15s on a cold/loaded runtime** — observed a single test hit 15,362ms. It blows vitest's 5s default. Tests that don't sign (safe omitted, fail-closed, schema-only) always pass.

Not a regression — `safety.test.ts` and every file it exercises were last touched only by the original safety feature and unrelated R2 work, never by recent cleanup PRs.

## Fix
- Add `{ timeout: 30000 }` to the two Bedrock-backed `describe` blocks.
- Pure test-budget change; no source or behavior change.

## Verification
3 isolated runs now green (21/21 each); slowest test 15.4s — comfortably under the new 30s ceiling, far over the old 5s.

## Risks
- None to product code. Only widens the per-test time budget. A genuine hang now surfaces at 30s instead of 5s (acceptable — these tests legitimately need >5s).

gist.headline: Giving tests room to breathe
gist.blurb: We bumped the timeout on our Bedrock safety tests so they stop failing when the crypto engine needs a second to warm up. No more flaky CI runs!
gist.summary: Increased the timeout for Bedrock-backed safety tests in gen.pollinations.ai from 5s to 30s to accommodate slow AWS SigV4 crypto operations on cold runtimes.
gist.impact: Developers will experience more stable CI pipelines with fewer flaky test failures during parallel runs.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee mascot holding a stopwatch next to a chunky retro server that is slowly warming up. Represents patience and extending time limits for tests.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot holding a tiny stopwatch, patiently waiting next to a chunky retro server that has a 'warming up' progress bar on its screen. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm and patient.
